content.problemStatement: Write a program for the coding challenge "Chocolates". Refer to LeetCode description: https://codeforces.com/problemset/problem/1139/B
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer n, which is the number of chocolates. The second line contains n space-separated integers representing the number of chocolates each child has.
content.outputFormat: The minimum number of operations required to make all children have the same number of chocolates.

hidden.label: tricky case / empty-like / negative if allowed

solution.approach: Find the median of the chocolates array, then calculate the minimum number of operations required to make all children have the same number of chocolates as the median.

code.Python3:
n = int(input())
chocolates = list(map(int, input().split()))
chocolates.sort()
median = chocolates[n // 2]
operations = sum(abs(chocolate - median) for chocolate in chocolates)
print(operations)
